He argues that consistency should not be a primary user interface goal .Grudin identifies three types of consistency : Internal consistency of the design , external consistency with other interfaces , and analogical or metaphoric consistency with features in the real world .

He analyzes several examples of physical and software interface design and demonstrates that giving primacy to the principle of consistency often produces sub-optimal designs .
